slides.title: Success, Careers, and Personal Growth
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 7: Success, Careers, and Personal Growth. Welcome to Unit 7: Success, Careers, and Personal Growth. In this unit you will explore what it means to succeed, how people build meaningful careers, and how personal growth shapes the choices we make. You will read about real-world career paths, discuss what makes a person truly successful, and discover that success is not only about money or fame — it is also about dedication, resilience, and the contribution you make to your community. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand a passage about careers and success, and discuss its ideas in English
Use adjective + preposition combinations correctly (e.g., *interested in*, *proud of*, *responsible for*)
Understand and use verb patterns with infinitives (*to do*) and gerunds (*doing*)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand a passage about careers and success, and discuss its ideas in english. you will also use adjective + preposition combinations correctly (e.g., *interested in*, *proud of*, *responsible for*). you will also understand and use verb patterns with infinitives (*to do*) and gerunds (*doing*). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: ambition, dedication, perseverance, achievement, milestone, distinction. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Success, Careers, and Personal Growth: source text 1
Success, Careers, and Personal Growth: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Success, Careers, and Personal Growth: source text 1 and Success, Careers, and Personal Growth: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Adjective + Preposition — The Basics
bullets: What it means and why it matters In English, many adjectives must be followed by a specific preposition. You cannot choose just any preposition — each...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Adjective + Preposition — The Basics. What it means and why it matters In English, many adjectives must be followed by a specific preposition. You cannot choose just any preposition — each adjective has a fixed partner. For example, we say interested in (not “interested about”) and proud of (not “proud for”). Learning these pairs will make your English sound natural and help you write accurately about success, careers, and personal growth. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
